hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. This will increase the selectivity margin by strengthening SSTR2 binding and weakening off-target binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 selectivity (Δmargin > 0), but structural quality remains a bottleneck. Focusing on improving structural validity and diversity will help converge on a viable, selective candidate.

proposed_changes.rationale: Low pass rate (25%) suggests insufficient diversity in candidate generation. Increasing the number of candidates will improve the chances of generating structurally valid designs.
Structural failures may be due to high clash scores. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce invalid structures.
